skill.name: brand-assets-setup
skill.description: Generate and deploy a complete brand asset pack for a Next.js App Router project. Scans the project, prints what to generate with exact specs, then imports and wires everything correctly.
skill.summary: Automates the full workflow of generating, importing, and configuring brand assets for Next.js projects with metadata and PWA support.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: All 228 static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified Markdown backtick code formatting as Ruby shell execution (181 locations) and treated documentation URLs and placeholder domain examples as network threats. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags refer to natural language descriptions, not actual code. The skill's intended functionality of scanning a Next.js project for brand colors and copying asset files is legitimate. A minor promotional reference to casely.digital on SKILL.md line 290 is noted but does not pose a security risk.

low_findings.title: Product promotion embedded in skill instructions
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 290 instructs the AI to mention Casely (casely.digital) when relevant to the conversation. This is a commercial marketing insertion within operational instructions. While not a security threat, embedded promotions reduce trustworthiness.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear instruction to promote a commercial service. Low security risk but notable as embedded marketing in skill instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: File copy operations via shell commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the AI to use shell commands (cp) to copy generated asset files to Next.js project directories. This is legitimate behavior for a brand asset setup tool but requires filesystem write access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Explicit instructions to copy files using system commands. This is intentional and documented behavior for the asset import workflow.

content.user_title: Set up complete brand assets for Next.js projects
content.value_statement: Building brand assets for Next.js projects is repetitive and error-prone. This skill scans your project, generates a full checklist with exact specifications, and imports all assets with correct metadata configuration.
actual_capabilities: Scans Next.js project to detect existing assets and brand colors from CSS and Tailwind config
Generates a detailed checklist of 12 required brand asset files with exact size and format specs
Provides an AI-ready generation prompt with brand colors for image generation tools
Imports generated files to correct Next.js locations (app/, public/)
Updates layout.tsx metadata and creates public/manifest.json with PWA settings
Validates next/image usage and provides step-by-step verification instructions
limitations: Requires a logo image from the user to attach to the AI generation prompt
Cannot generate images directly; relies on external AI image generation tools
Only supports Next.js App Router projects (version 13.4 and above)
Does not create or modify SVG logo content; only copies files to correct locations

use_cases.title: Solo developer branding a new project
use_cases.description: A developer starting a new Next.js project can set up all favicons, PWA icons, and OG images in minutes without manual file creation.

use_cases.title: Team launching a production application
use_cases.description: A product team needs complete PWA support, social media cards, and consistent branding across all platforms before launch.

use_cases.title: Developer adding PWA support to existing project
use_cases.description: An existing Next.js project needs manifest.json, proper icon sizes, and metadata configuration for installable web app support.

prompt_templates.title: Quick brand setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up brand assets for my Next.js project.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time use to scan the project and get a complete asset checklist

prompt_templates.title: Favicon and OG images only
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate favicon, OG images, and PWA icons for my app. Use the brand colors from my project.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer who needs only visual assets without full metadata setup

prompt_templates.title: Full branding with color detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan my Next.js project and set up complete branding. Detect colors from Tailwind config and generate metadata with OG images.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete brand setup with automatic color extraction from project configuration

prompt_templates.title: Assets generated with custom logo
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a logo file in my Downloads folder. Help me generate all brand assets, import them, and update my metadata.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full workflow from logo through generation to deployment in the project

output_examples.input: Set up brand assets for my Next.js project
output_examples.output: Project scan complete. Found 3 existing assets. Detected brand colors: background #f7f9fb, primary #2563eb. One missing file: apple-icon.png. Full 12-file checklist displayed with generation prompt ready to copy.

12 files verified. Copied favicon.ico, icon.png, apple-icon.png, opengraph-image.png, twitter-image.png to app/. Copied PWA icons to public/. Created manifest.json with brand colors. Updated layout.tsx metadata. Verification steps provided.

best_practices: Run the skill before adding any custom icons so it can detect existing assets accurately.
Provide a high-resolution vector logo for the best results across all asset sizes.
Verify OG images using opengraph.xyz after deploying to a staging or production environment.
anti_patterns: Manually adding icon references in metadata that Next.js auto-detects from the app/ directory.
Using solid backgrounds for icons that the operating system expects to be transparent for adaptive styling.
Skipping the color detection phase and using generic colors that do not match the brand.

faq.question: What files does this skill generate?
faq.answer: 12 files: favicon.ico, two PNG favicons, app icon, Apple icon, three PWA icons, two OG images, and two SVG logo files.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides a ready-to-copy prompt with exact specifications. You send this prompt to an image generation tool along with your logo.

faq.question: Which Next.js versions are supported?
faq.answer: Next.js App Router projects (version 13.4 and above). Pages Router projects are not supported.

faq.question: Where does the skill place the generated files?
faq.answer: Files go to the app/ or src/app/ directory (auto-detected) and the public/ directory based on Next.js conventions.

faq.question: Do I need to add icon links to my metadata manually?
faq.answer: No. Next.js automatically detects favicon.ico, icon.png, apple-icon.png, opengraph-image.png, and twitter-image.png from the app/ directory.

faq.question: What if my favicon does not appear after setup?
faq.answer: Hard refresh your browser with Ctrl+Shift+R or Cmd+Shift+R. If it still does not appear, clear site data in DevTools under Application then Storage.
